title: zsh plugin stack (guarded, profile-aware)
outcome: Guarded, profile-aware zsh plugin stack added at dot_config/zsh/plugins.zsh.tmpl (bare zsh; no Oh My Zsh / Powerlevel10k; Starship prompt retained). zsh-autosuggestions (MIT) + zsh-syntax-highlighting (BSD-3, sourced LAST); zsh-autocomplete (MIT) opt-in via WFOS_ZSH_AUTOCOMPLETE on full/maintainer only; agent-safe loads no interactive plugins. dot_zshrc sources the fragment and sets PANOPLY_PLUGINS_MANAGED=1; panoply.zsh's plugin block was wrapped to stand down when that flag is set (additive, default behavior unchanged) to prevent double-sourcing.
validation: validate.sh PASS — fragment sources both required plugins, highlighting after autosuggestions, sourcing guarded, agent-safe opt-out present, no heavy-framework artifacts, panoply.zsh respects PANOPLY_PLUGINS_MANAGED; bash -n clean on panoply.zsh; chezmoi-render DEFERRED (binary not installed)
decisions: chezmoi plugin fragment is the single authority in the chezmoi wiring path; panoply.zsh stands down via the new opt-in PANOPLY_PLUGINS_MANAGED guard (additive edit to existing panoply.zsh, default behavior preserved)
zsh-autocomplete kept optional + opt-in (conflict risk) and excluded from headless-dev/agent-safe
install of the plugins remains E03's responsibility; this story delivered only the shell integration
token_note: loaded 1 story block + 1 canon ref (native-toolchain) + STATE + ledger tail; compact PASS/FAIL gate output instead of raw shell logs
